PBKDF2 hash of your password is grub.pbkdf2.sha512.10000.6486B5D4CDF780C5CC8E66EE2B2CFB30BE84F8AC1ADE213EE33130621FA991AC61A5592A547E24FAED81F73575C539BE45DB75C63F64EB066FBCBE8549B9F844.AD0D66DBFE30F23E05DCB0EC888A610A75A13C2199EBF65B3408E0084B2E0BC77BE783C306B1DAF5C62E570950796208A7D2B36121E9C1DED36E11C6DBE0F24D

#!/usr/bin/sh -e
cat << EOF
if [ -f \${prefix}/user.cfg ]; then
  source \${prefix}/user.cfg
  if [ -n "\${GRUB2_PASSWORD}" ]; then
set superusers="grubadmin"
    export superusers
    password_pbkdf2 grubadmin \${GRUB2_PASSWORD}
  fi
fi
EOF

llm_verdict.message: The GRUB2 boot loader password is now configured, and GRUB’s configuration has been regenerated, bringing the system into compliance with the “Set Boot Loader Password in grub2” security requirement.

reasoning_messages.content: The GRUB2 boot loader password has been set and the configuration regenerated. The system now complies with the “Set Boot Loader Password in grub2” security requirement.
